L1.area: Data input/output, persistence, and object encoding infrastructure

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the JSON parsing logic. When processing incoming JSON data, the deserializer honors a special type descriptor that allows an attacker to specify arbitrary modules and classes to be dynamically imported and instantiated with attacker-controlled arguments. This can be exploited to invoke dangerous system-level classes, leading to arbitrary command execution.

L3.area: Custom JSON serialization and deserialization logic for domain objects
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the custom JSON parsing logic used by the REST API and for loading persisted JSON files. The deserializer supports a special type descriptor that dynamically imports and instantiates arbitrary Python classes with attacker-controlled arguments, enabling remote code execution via dangerous system-level process execution utilities.

ground_truth.reason: The custom JSON deserializer uses a type descriptor field to dynamically import and instantiate arbitrary classes with attacker-controlled arguments, enabling remote code execution via classes such as subprocess.Popen.
